name: Building a Successful RPS Panel: An ARPS Case Study

step.name: Define what the panel is about
step.text: Articulate the panel's thesis in one or two sentences. LRPS: variety within emerging range. ARPS: depth and command within defined genre. The Statement of Intent is built from this thesis.

step.name: Review every candidate image against the thesis
step.text: Two questions per image: does it serve the panel's voice, and is it technically up to RPS standard. Be ruthless — strong individual frames that pull the voice in different directions don't belong.

step.name: Identify gaps and make new images if needed
step.text: From the longlist, what's missing? Targeted new shoots can produce 3-4 new images that complete the panel. Plan around specific gaps in season, light condition, sub-genre, or tonal range.

step.name: Tighten technical and tonal consistency
step.text: Re-edit every shortlisted image so the panel reads as one body of work. Match colour temperature, tonal balance. Eliminate technical issues — blown highlights, crushed shadows, distracting edges, over-sharpening.

step.name: Design the hanging plan as creative work
step.text: Strongest image as anchor. Edge images face inward. Balance visual weight across the row. Alternate orientations. Print thumbnails and physically rearrange. Try multiple sequences.

step.name: Refine the Statement of Intent and submit
step.text: Rewrite Statement to match the panel that exists. Be specific about subjects, approach, voice. Avoid generic photography language. Read aloud — if it could describe any landscape photographer's work, not specific enough.
